body:
**Scope:** add the two `ChunkStore` affordances M1/M2 deliberately left out (0003: *"a `list`/`has` affordance may be added when M2's networked discovery needs it"*). The trait is `put_fragment`/`get_fragment`/`health` only (`traits/src/lib.rs:72-84`) — a store cannot be **walked** (scrub needs it) and a fragment cannot be **deleted** (GC needs it; today `sweep_expired_leases` deletes ledger entries but **no bytes**). Add `list_fragments(&self) -> Result<Vec<FragmentId>>` and `delete_fragment(&self, id) -> Result<()>` to the trait, the gRPC `ChunkStore` service (`ListFragments`/`DeleteFragment`, additive — fields never repurposed, ADR-0002/§8.7), and both backends (`chunkstore-fs` directory walk; `chunkstore-grpc` rpcs). The D server stays deliberately dumb (§8.5).

**Definition of done**
- [ ] `list_fragments` enumerates a store's fragments over real tonic and in-process
- [ ] `delete_fragment` removes bytes over real tonic and in-process
- [ ] proto service evolution is additive (one-version-gap interop); `cargo-deny` green
- [ ] existing round-trip put/get/health unaffected

**Needs:** — (parallel with M3.1). **Refs:** proposal 0005 §"`ChunkStore`: enumerate + delete"; architecture §8.5, §8.7.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: M3.2 — ChunkStore: list_fragments + delete_fragment (enumerate for scrub, delete for GC)
